structured.name: Internal Load Balancing
structured.objective: To enable internal load balancing within the Charging Station and between EVSEs.
structured.description:
The Load Balancing use case is about internal load balancing within the Charging Station, where
the Charging Station controls current/power per EVSE.
The Charging Station is configured with a fixed limit, e.g. the maximum current of the connection
to the grid.
See K01 - Set Charging Profile
scenario: 1. The CSMS sets known physical grid connection limits by sending a ChargingProfile.
2. The Charging Station controls current/power per EVSE.
3. The EVSE sends a Control Pilot signal to the EV.
structured.prerequisites: The Functional Block Smart Charging is installed.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station Successfully balances the current/power between the different EVSEs,
based on what the CSMS is sending.
Failure postcondition:
ChargingProfile is not Accepted. Charging is possible, although the Charging Station will not
adhere to the ChargingProfile.

requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL control the
ChargingSchedule per EVSE.

The Charging Station SHALL be configured with a fixed
limit.

A ChargingProfile with the purpose
ChargingStationMaxProfile can only be set at
Charging Station EVSE with Id 0.

The optional ChargingSchedule field minChargingRate
MAY be used by the Charging Station to optimize the
power distribution between the EVSEs.

The combined energy flow of all EVSEs (and the Charging
Station hardware itself) SHALL NOT be greater than the
limit set by ChargingStationMaxProfile.

chunks.content:
# K04 - Internal Load Balancing

**Objective:** To enable internal load balancing within the Charging Station and between EVSEs.

**Description:** The Load Balancing use case is about internal load balancing within the Charging Station, where
the Charging Station controls current/power per EVSE.
The Charging Station is configured with a fixed limit, e.g. the maximum current of the connection
to the grid.
See K01 - Set Charging Profile

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The CSMS sets known physical grid connection limits by sending a ChargingProfile.
2. The Charging Station controls current/power per EVSE.
3. The EVSE sends a Control Pilot signal to the EV.

**Prerequisites:** The Functional Block Smart Charging is installed.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station Successfully balances the current/power between the different EVSEs,
based on what the CSMS is sending.
Failure postcondition:
ChargingProfile is not Accepted. Charging is possible, although the Charging Station will not
adhere to the ChargingProfile.

# K04 - Requirements

**K04.FR.01**
The Charging Station SHALL control the
ChargingSchedule per EVSE.

**K04.FR.02**
The Charging Station SHALL be configured with a fixed
limit.

**K04.FR.03**
A ChargingProfile with the purpose
ChargingStationMaxProfile can only be set at
Charging Station EVSE with Id 0.

**K04.FR.04**
The optional ChargingSchedule field minChargingRate
MAY be used by the Charging Station to optimize the
power distribution between the EVSEs.

**K04.FR.05**
The combined energy flow of all EVSEs (and the Charging
Station hardware itself) SHALL NOT be greater than the
limit set by ChargingStationMaxProfile.
